steps.title: Connect the form to f of one
steps.content: The value $f(1)$ appears as a coefficient when the exponential expression is written with exponent $x-1$.

steps.title: Identify the matching form
steps.content:
In the form
$$f(x)=98(1.4)^{x-1}$$substituting $x=1$ makes the exponent $0$.
$$f(1)=98(1.4)^0=98$$So this form shows $k$ as the coefficient. The answer is C.
